jokes.text: Milford School credo: children should be 'neither seen nor heard' - Woman.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains prestigious school's old-fashioned credo about silent children
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by a woman loudly contradicting the silence
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and irony - the school's silence rule immediately undercut by loud interruption. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I don't understand the question, and I won't respond to it.
jokes.setup: Waitress asks simple 'plate or platter' question about tuna
jokes.punchline: Lucille's haughty, dramatically overwrought non-response to basic question
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Lucille - perfectly captures her pretentious character with absurd overreaction to mundane question. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'd like my old life back.
jokes.setup: Waitress asks what she'd like to order
jokes.punchline: Lindsay responds with existential despair instead of food order
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's self-pity, but relatively straightforward sad-rich-person joke.

jokes.text: Everything I've said about you can be covered with makeup and a lie about a thyroid problem.
jokes.setup: Lindsay confronts Lucille about treating her badly
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casual cruelty disguised as practical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille darkness - dismisses emotional damage as cosmetic problems. Shows her complete lack of maternal instinct.

jokes.text: Sorry, Lindsay. There goes your dessert.
jokes.setup: Family argument escalating at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Waitress matter-of-factly denying dessert due to behavior
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing external consequences of family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Buster so excelled at being neither seen nor heard that he remained at the school undetected for a full two semesters after he was supposed to graduate.
jokes.setup: Reference back to Milford School's 'neither seen nor heard' philosophy
jokes.punchline: Buster was so good at invisibility he accidentally overstayed by two semesters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that escalates the Milford concept and perfectly fits Buster's invisible personality. Great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Is this your onion? / Yes. / What's in the foil? / Nothing. It's a ball of foil for my son.
jokes.setup: Security checking Lucille's purse after restaurant incident
jokes.punchline: Lucille's 'lunch' consists of a raw onion and literal garbage
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing the family's poverty through absurd 'lunch.' Good character beat for Lucille's delusions.

jokes.text: Race you to the top of the hill. / I'll race you to the top of the hill. / I brought a picnic lunch.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to flirt at the gym
jokes.punchline: His awkward, repetitive attempts and non-sequitur about picnic lunch
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy showing Michael's rusty flirting skills, but fairly standard awkward-dating material.

jokes.text: My relations are already a little too public.
jokes.setup: Jessie mentions 'public relations needs'
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-aware pun about his family's public scandals
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that acknowledges the show's premise, but fairly obvious pun.

jokes.text: She's a cutie. She's a little cutie pie. But I never really noticed.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he never noticed if Jessie was cute
jokes.punchline: His increasingly elaborate descriptions while claiming not to notice
jokes.explanation: Standard contradiction comedy - the escalating adjectives while denying interest works but isn't particularly novel.

jokes.text: I have a job. / Kiss ass. / Well, we were all thinking it.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming he has a job
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's blunt dismissal and Lucille's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Lindsay's directness followed by Lucille's casual cruelty. Efficient character work.

jokes.text: Kiss ass. Well, we were all thinking it.
jokes.setup: Gob calling George Michael a kiss ass
jokes.punchline: Lucille matter-of-factly agrees on everyone's behalf
jokes.explanation: Sharp family dynamic moment with Lucille's brutal honesty delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: When did you get a job? / At the banana stand. / Oh! Duh. I thought you meant like a plumber or something.
jokes.setup: Michael confused about George Michael having a job
jokes.punchline: Forgetting about the family business and expecting manual labor from his teenage son
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Michael's scattered attention and absurd expectations for George Michael.

jokes.text: Yeah. Who doesn't love the Jews?
jokes.setup: Discussing George Sr.'s Torah study as sympathetic
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's oblivious comment about antisemitism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Lindsay's obliviousness to historical context.

jokes.text: Who doesn't love the Jews?
jokes.setup: Discussion of George Sr.'s religious conversion being good PR
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casually tone-deaf comment about Jewish people
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille obliviousness - treats Judaism as a marketing demographic. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, that certainly leaves me out. / She... She said single.
jokes.setup: Jessie lists qualities: intelligent, attractive, straight, single men
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinks 'straight' eliminates him, Michael thinks 'single' eliminates him
jokes.explanation: Subtle character development - Tobias accidentally reveals his sexual confusion while Michael misses the point entirely.

jokes.text: to the jackal as to an oxen. Did it get a laugh?
jokes.setup: George Sr. doing Torah study in prison
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to ask if his line got a laugh
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment showing George Sr.'s performative religious conversion.

jokes.text: You know that he's only had sex with, like, four women, right?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Michael being the most likable family member
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's casual oversharing of Michael's private sexual history
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lindsay's inappropriate boundary-crossing and competitive nature with her brother.

jokes.text: I truly believe that the universe wants me to be an actor and not a doctor. I'm just waiting for a sign. / Here's your cash. / Universal Shuttle picks you up at 8:00. / Any sign, really.
jokes.setup: Tobias looking for a sign from the universe about his career
jokes.punchline: Universe provides obvious 'Universal' shuttle sign which he ignores
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with 'Universal' and nice character work showing Tobias's selective perception and desperation.

jokes.text: Not wag our genitals at one another to make a point.
jokes.setup: George Sr. discussing Torah study with students
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate anatomical reference during religious discussion
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur from prison that contrasts with the religious context. Mildly cheap shock humor.

jokes.text: You can always tell a Milford man.
jokes.setup: Buster told to stay out of spotlight and be neither seen nor heard
jokes.punchline: Buster completely disappears from view, demonstrating Milford training
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff to the Milford setup. Buster's invisibility skills demonstrated with perfect economy. Highly quotable catchphrase.

jokes.text: Why would anybody pay more than $12 to go to the airport?
jokes.setup: Tobias pleased with cheap airport shuttle
jokes.punchline: His naivety about what he's actually boarded
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the reveal that he's on the wrong transport. Shows Tobias's cluelessness about practical matters.

jokes.text: You are Carl Weathers, the actor. / I went to San Francisco to attend your stage fighting workshop. / But you never showed up. / I got bumped from that flight.
jokes.setup: Tobias recognizing Carl Weathers and mentioning missed workshop
jokes.punchline: Carl's casual admission he didn't show up to his own workshop
jokes.explanation: Introduces Carl Weathers' character trait of being unreliable while setting up the scam relationship.

jokes.text: You are Carl Weathers, the actor.
jokes.setup: Tobias on the wrong bus/shuttle
jokes.punchline: Recognizing Carl Weathers from previous failed encounter
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Carl Weathers subplot with callback to earlier failed interaction.

jokes.text: Hell, they'll give you $300 if you get bumped. It's this crazy loophole in the system that the wrong guy discovered.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining why getting bumped from flights is good
jokes.punchline: His view that he's the 'wrong guy' to discover this money-making scheme
jokes.explanation: Establishes Carl's character as a penny-pinching schemer who sees himself as undeserving of good fortune.

jokes.text: $1,100 is exactly what I charge for acting classes. / No, it isn't! / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Carl claiming his acting classes cost exactly what Tobias has
jokes.punchline: Tobias calling out the obvious lie, Carl doubling down anyway
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Carl's shameless opportunism and Tobias's inability to recognize obvious scams.

jokes.text: $1,100 is exactly what I charge for acting classes. - No, it isn't! - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning he has $1,100
jokes.punchline: Carl immediately making up that as his exact price
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Carl's opportunism and Tobias's gullibility. Quick, well-structured exchange.

jokes.text: Universe, you've done it again.
jokes.setup: Tobias previously asking universe for a sign
jokes.punchline: Taking Carl's obvious scam as another sign from the universe
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Tobias's selective perception. The irony that he's being scammed makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A tomb? / Or box. Box is... fine.
jokes.setup: Gob performing magic trick called 'Aztec Tomb' at nursing home
jokes.punchline: Elderly person's concerning reaction and Gob's quick backpedal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about death anxiety at nursing home. Gob's awkward damage control shows his social cluelessness.

jokes.text: What? / He's gone.
jokes.setup: Gob performing magic trick with elderly volunteer
jokes.punchline: The volunteer has actually disappeared (presumably died)
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag that takes the magic trick premise to its logical extreme. Simple but effective shock humor.

jokes.text: Wow! This Cloudmir's making me think fuzzy. I have almost no judgment at all. / 'Only one with a real job.'
jokes.setup: Lindsay doing promotional work for vodka at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her fake enthusiasm followed by bitter aside about Michael's comment
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Lindsay's resentment and the absurdity of her 'job.' The callback adds layers.

jokes.text: A date? This is a date now? I thought this was just business. / Can't it be a little bit of both? / How will I know which part of it is business?
jokes.setup: Michael confused when photographer mentions them being on a date
jokes.punchline: His panicked overanalysis of the business/personal boundary
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward-dating comedy but fits Michael's overthinking character well.

jokes.text: Show me business again.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to understand business vs. date distinction
jokes.punchline: His attempt to literally demonstrate different modes
jokes.explanation: Escalates the awkwardness nicely with Michael's literal approach to the metaphorical question.

jokes.text: So when those guys kept saying, 'Hey, you, boy,' that was me? I was the boy?
jokes.setup: TV accidentally connected to prison feed
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent realization about prison catcalling
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag setup with innocent character reaction to disturbing implication.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I tried that earlier. I think it's still hooked up to the prison. / So when those guys kept saying, 'Hey, you, boy,' that was me? I was the boy?
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to fix TV at model home
jokes.punchline: Realization he was being addressed by prisoners through video link
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates retroactive humor and shows the family's technical dysfunction.

jokes.text: You and I are so different. It's like we're not even related. / That'd be amazing.
jokes.setup: George Michael noting differences between him and Maeby
jokes.punchline: Maeby's enthusiastic response to not being related
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their mutual attraction despite being cousins. Shows the show's willingness to go to uncomfortable places.

jokes.text: Bluth boys. / Bluth boys.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to bond with son after romantic confusion
jokes.punchline: Forced father-son catchphrase to assert their partnership
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment showing Michael's overcompensation and George Michael's polite participation in forced bonding.

jokes.text: So the two months on the bike, that was, what, for my health?
jokes.setup: Michael saying they should keep relationship professional
jokes.punchline: Jessie revealing she's been playing the long game with elaborate setup
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes their entire relationship. Shows Jessie as more calculating than initially appeared.

jokes.text: No. I was just saying your name as you walked away. I didn't... I have no follow-up.
jokes.setup: Michael calling after Jessie as she storms off
jokes.punchline: His honest admission that he has no plan or clever comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - his honesty about social incompetence is both endearing and funny. Great deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Murder. - Who died? - My career.
jokes.setup: Gob dramatically announcing murder
jokes.punchline: Metaphorical murder of his career, not actual murder
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with good setup/payoff structure and typical Gob self-absorption.

jokes.text: Although he was spectacularly quiet in there. / You can always tell a Milford man.
jokes.setup: Gob describing the missing Earl Milford from magic trick
jokes.punchline: Narrator's callback suggesting his silence was due to Milford training, not death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that works on multiple levels - pays off Milford setup while making light of potential death. Perfectly timed and quotable.

jokes.text: Although he was spectacularly quiet in there. You can always tell a Milford man.
jokes.setup: Earl Milford disappeared during magic trick
jokes.punchline: His silence could be death OR perfect Milford training
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark callback that works on multiple levels - could be death or perfect training.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'm cut out to be a... a De Niro... or a Regis or a Pinkett-Smith or what have you.
jokes.setup: Tobias doubting his acting abilities
jokes.punchline: His random list including a game show host and partial celebrity name
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's confused understanding of celebrity culture and acting aspirations. Character-specific but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I don't know if I'm cut out to be a... a De Niro or a Regis or a Pinkett-Smith or what have you.
jokes.setup: Tobias expressing acting doubts
jokes.punchline: Random mix of actor names including inappropriate examples
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's confused understanding of the acting world.

jokes.text: Your wife works in a restaurant? Do they get a shift meal... or do they just pay half-price on select menu items?
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning his wife works at a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Carl's immediate interest in the food benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carl Weathers moment showing his penny-pinching priorities. The specific detail about meal options is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And don't edit this for your broadcast so it looks like I'm screaming... 'I killed Earl Milford.' / Startling confession, tonight at 11:00.
jokes.setup: Gob specifically asking news not to edit his statement to look like confession
jokes.punchline: Immediate news tease doing exactly what he asked them not to do
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate payoff showing media manipulation. The specific nature of Gob's request makes the reversal funnier.

jokes.text: Don't send me back. They abuse us. It's not pleasant. / Earl, I have to take you back. It's the only way to clear my name. / You'll have a new name: Hero.
jokes.setup: Earl begging not to return to nursing home
jokes.punchline: Earl's sales pitch that Gob would be called 'Hero'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nursing home conditions combined with appeal to Gob's ego. Works well for both characters.

jokes.text: Hero. / That could work for me.
jokes.setup: Earl suggesting Gob would be called 'Hero'
jokes.punchline: Gob's immediate consideration of the ego appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment showing how easily his ego can be manipulated. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: You'll have a new name: Hero. - Hero. - That could work for me.
jokes.setup: Earl suggesting Gob will be seen as hero for exposing abuse
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately sold on the ego boost
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's immediate attraction to positive attention.

jokes.text: Daddy lost his shot at happy, and it's all your fault, Opie.
jokes.setup: Jessie confronting George Michael about interfering with his father's love life
jokes.punchline: Calling him 'Opie' while blaming a child for adult relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Shows Jessie's unprofessional behavior and willingness to blame a child. The 'Opie' reference is dated but fits her character.

jokes.text: She kind of reminds me of my mother.
jokes.setup: Jessie being cruel and manipulative to George Michael
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact comparison to Lucille
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight that connects Jessie's manipulative behavior to family patterns. Shows George Michael's awareness.

jokes.text: You're married to Carl Weathers?
jokes.setup: Lindsay being approached by man at restaurant, then Tobias arriving with Carl
jokes.punchline: Man's assumption about the relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke about relationship assumptions, though fairly straightforward.

jokes.text: I buy all my cars at police auction.
jokes.setup: Carl sharing life lessons with Tobias
jokes.punchline: Another example of his penny-pinching philosophy
jokes.explanation: Another Carl Weathers money-saving tip that builds his character consistently. Good economy of words.

jokes.text: I don't need my son reading about us in the paper. / Oh, you and your son. Stop using him as an excuse for everything you do.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to protect George Michael from bad publicity
jokes.punchline: Jessie calling out his pattern of using his son as excuse
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Michael's defensive patterns, though the observation itself isn't particularly funny.

jokes.text: Food fight!
jokes.setup: Confrontation between Jessie and the Bluth women
jokes.punchline: Lucille's childish declaration starting physical altercation
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille immaturity but fairly standard slapstick setup. The announcement of 'food fight' is charmingly juvenile.

jokes.text: I didn't want to go to that school, and Jessie's a psycho.
jokes.setup: Michael asking George Michael to be honest about his feelings
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment from George Michael. His casual 'psycho' diagnosis is both accurate and funny.

jokes.text: Four women, that is kind of sad. / All right. Thank you. / I know you got married in college, but that was your sophomore year. You had your whole freshman year to...
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to be supportive about Michael dating
jokes.punchline: His oversharing analysis of Michael's sexual history timeline
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows George Michael absorbed the family's inappropriate oversharing. His mathematical approach is funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: He's resting, so... Oh.
jokes.setup: Gob at press conference to reveal Earl Milford is alive
jokes.punchline: Earl appears to have died again during the conference
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag that escalates the Earl Milford situation. Gob's understated reaction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He's resting, so... Oh. Pick a card. Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Gob at press conference to reveal Earl is alive
jokes.punchline: Earl has died during the press conference, Gob awkwardly continues magic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation with perfect Gob character work - continuing to perform despite crisis.
